nonBlockingIssues.description: vLLM monkey-patching implementation is incomplete - the turboquant_forward method stores original result but doesn't actually use TurboQuant compression in the attention computation
nonBlockingIssues.suggestion: Complete the integration by properly decompressing keys and using compute_attention_with_compressed_keys

nonBlockingIssues.description: convert_hf_to_gguf method relies on external convert_hf_to_gguf.py script which may not be available
nonBlockingIssues.suggestion: Consider adding fallback to ctransformers or transformers' built-in GGUF export if available

appliedUpdates.description: Fixed QJL projection scaling factor from 1/sqrt(input_dim) to 1/sqrt(proj_dim) to correctly preserve inner products
Updated test comments and added proper scaling explanation for QJL projection inner product test
Added type ignore comment for mypy compatibility with optional vllm imports
Fixed type annotations and import handling for optional llama-cpp-python

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note about handling optional dependencies in Python 3.14+ where some packages (like vLLM) may not be available. Pattern: use try/except imports and provide stub implementations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both vllm-backend-adapter and llama-cpp-backend-adapter use this pattern successfully.

library.value: vLLM backend handles Python 3.14+ gracefully by providing a stub implementation that raises informative errors. Pattern: check VLLM_AVAILABLE constant before importing vLLM-specific classes.
llama.cpp backend handles missing llama-cpp-python gracefully with stub implementation. Pattern: check LLAMA_CPP_AVAILABLE constant before importing.
TurboQuant KV cache compression uses QJL (Quantized Johnson-Lindenstrauss) projection with Lloyd-Max quantization. Key compression is 3-bit, value compression is 2-bit.
Johnson-Lindenstrauss projection for QJL uses scaling factor 1/sqrt(proj_dim) to preserve inner products in expectation.
GGUF model loading supports both direct file paths and HuggingFace model IDs. For HF models, searches for pre-converted GGUF variants from TheBloke and others.
